input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.output_format: Return `mid_output` as one non-empty string containing a summary1 output.

task_context.question: What was the name of Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 album released by EMI Records?
task_context.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

left_state.output:
Primary topic: Red Hot Chili Peppers — EMI releases

- "Sock-Cess" — "1989 compilation album" by Red Hot Chili Peppers, "released exclusively in the UK by EMI" as a "promotional only release."  
- "The Red Hot Chili Peppers" (debut) — released August 10, 1984 on "EMI Records."  
- Missing bridge fact: none — passages explicitly confirm the 1989 EMI release is "Sock-Cess"; no additional retrieval needed.  
- Optional next hops (low priority): open "Sock-Cess" page lede/infobox and "Red Hot Chili Peppers discography" → "Albums" section to cross-check release listing.

hop2_doc_snippets: Sock-Cess | Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release.  The album contains tracks from the band's first four studio albums.

Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers | Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers is a compilation album released on March 31, 1998 by the Red Hot Chili Peppers.  All of the songs featured are covers mainly from the band's early albums.

John Frusciante discography | The discography of John Frusciante, who is best known as the former guitarist of the rock band Red Hot Chili Peppers, consists of eleven solo albums and four EPs, as well as two albums with collaborators Joe Lally and Josh Klinghoffer under the name Ataxia and one EP and one album under the alias of Trickfinger.  Frusciante left the Red Hot Chili Peppers in 1992 after he got tired of the growing success of the band.  He released his first solo album, "Niandra LaDes  ...[truncated]

List of songs recorded by Red Hot Chili Peppers | A complete list of known songs written and recorded by the Red Hot Chili Peppers that have been released or have gone unreleased.  The list also includes the full discographies of all of the band members, past and current.
Can't Stop (Red Hot Chili Peppers song) | "Can't Stop" is a song by the Red Hot Chili Peppers from their 2002 album, "By the Way".  It is the third single released from the album.

The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) | The Red Hot Chili Peppers is the debut studio album by American rock band Red Hot Chili Peppers, released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records.  The album was produced by Gang of Four guitarist Andy Gill, and is the only album to feature Jack Sherman on guitar.  Sherman was fired by the band after the album release tour, replaced by founding member Hillel Slovak. " Get Up and Jump" was the only single released from the album, but a music video was made for the so ...[truncated]

ITunes Originals – Red Hot Chili Peppers | iTunes Originals – Red Hot Chili Peppers is a virtual album by Red Hot Chili Peppers which was released only on the iTunes Store as part of the "iTunes Originals" series of digital albums.  It features live versions of the band playing some of their more famous songs as well as featuring the band themselves talking about their careers.  It comprises videos of their live performances for the iTunes Originals as well as interviews about their careers, fea ...[truncated]
left_state.summary_2: "Sock-Cess" — "1989 compilation album" by Red Hot Chili Peppers, "released exclusively in the UK by EMI" as a "promotional only release." Sock-Cess contains tracks from the band's first four studio albums.

right_state.output:
Summary of first-hop evidence:
- Hop1 returned a page for Sock-Cess stating: "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release." It also notes the compilation pulls tracks from the band's first four studio albums.
- Hop1 also returned the band’s debut album page: The Red Hot Chili Peppers (1984) — released on EMI Records (useful to show other EMI associations).

Bridge / missing fact needed for next hop:
- Clarify whether the question’s target is that 1989 EMI promotional compilation (Sock-Cess) or a different 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers album (e.g., a studio release) that was issued on EMI. In short: we need to confirm the canonical 1989 album title and its label/territory attribution.

Compact retrieval direction for the next agent (query agent):
- Open the Sock-Cess page (lede/infobox) to confirm release date, label, and region/format (promotional vs. standard release).
- Retrieve the Mother’s Milk page and the Good Time Boys page to check their release years and label credits (these titles were returned as possible related hits and are listed as missing retrieval targets).
- If available, open the band’s discography page (Albums section) to cross-check which album(s) from 1989 are listed and what labels/territories are shown.

Notes: do not assume or state a final answer here — next hop should confirm which 1989 release the question refers to and verify its EMI release details.
right_state.hop2_query: Sock-Cess Mothers Milk Good Time Boys label
hop2_doc_snippets: Good Time Boys | "Good Time Boys" is a song by the Red Hot Chili Peppers and the first song from their 1989 album, "Mother's Milk".  The song contains excerpts of "Bonin' in the Boneyard" by Fishbone, "Try" by Thelonious Monster and "White Girl" by X.  The song is also notable for including backing vocals by the band's former guitarist, Jack Sherman who was fired in 1984.

Sock-Cess | Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release.  The album contains tracks from the band's first four studio albums.

The Boys (The Necks album) | The Boys is the sixth album and first soundtrack album by Australian improvised music trio The Necks originally released on the Wild Sound/MDS label in 1998 and later rereleased on Fish of Milk.  The album features music recorded for the Australian motion picture "The Boys" (1998).  The All About Jazz review states that "The album would make a good start for those who want to get into these Australians' music, because it's made of smaller doses of the trio's traditio ...[truncated]

Every Good Boy Deserves Fudge | Every Good Boy Deserves Fudge is the second studio album by the grunge band Mudhoney.  It was recorded in 1991, at a time when the band was thinking of signing to a major record label, but decided to release the album on Sub Pop.  Guitarist Steve Turner has said that the album is his "favorite Mudhoney album as a whole" and many critics agree that the band reached a peak on "Every Good Boy Deserves Fudge".

Boy Krazy (album) | Boy Krazy is the self-titled debut of girl group Boy Krazy.  This was the only album the group made.  It was produced by Mike Stock and Pete Waterman, except "That's What Love Can Do" and "Good Times with Bad Boys", which are a Stock Aitken & Waterman production.  Each member sings the lead vocals on at least two songs on the album.  The album includes many tracks originally written for Kylie Minogue had Minogue stayed on Stock Aitken & Waterman's PWL label.

Good Time (The Beach Boys song) | "Good Time" is a song written by Brian Wilson and Al Jardine for the American rock band the Beach Boys.  The instrumental track and vocals were recorded by the group in early 1970 but was left unreleased by the group until the 1977 album "Love You".  In 1972 American pop duo American Spring released "Good Time" as their second single, recording their voices atop the Beach Boys' instrumental track.

Good Times with Bad Boys | "Good Times with Bad Boys" is a single by American female pop group Boy Krazy, written and produced by British team Stock Aitken & Waterman.  Lead vocals were performed by group members Kimberly Blake and Johnna Cummings.
right_state.summary_2: Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release. Mother's Milk is also a 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers album (the provided passages do not state its label).

request.rendered_prompt:
You are working with a functional semantic distance over states of one
agent in a multi-agent pipeline.

Each state contains the selected agent's output and may also contain the
downstream consequences produced by that output while the rest of the
pipeline is held fixed. The agent output is the primary object of the
transition. Downstream evidence and metrics are supporting evidence about
its functional effect.

The distance is not surface text edit distance and is not a correctness
score. It measures how much task-relevant behavior and information must
change to move from one state to another.

Judge distance using:
1. Agent-output behavior: what the output is trying to accomplish for the
   selected agent's role.
2. Task-relevant information: which entities, relations, evidence,
   conclusions, or answer constraints are preserved, added, removed, or
   corrected.
3. Downstream consequence: how the output changes retrieval, evidence,
   answerability, or information available to later agents.
4. Transition utility: whether a state is a meaningful intermediate step
   between two endpoint states.

Do not infer distance from text length, token count, document count, or
lexical overlap. Do not treat a state as closer merely because its metric
or final answer is better. Metrics are evidence of functional consequence,
not the distance definition itself.

A midpoint output o_mid between o_left and o_right must:
- preserve useful task-relevant behavior or information from o_left;
- make concrete semantic progress toward o_right;
- differ meaningfully from both endpoint outputs;
- not merely paraphrase or copy o_left;
- not simply copy o_right;
- represent two smaller functional transitions than the original edge.

Generate one midpoint output between the left and right projected states.
The midpoint output will be used directly as the destination output for a
local prompt-update inference step. Do not output a prompt update.

Make the smallest meaningful functional change from the left output toward
the right output. Adopt at least one task-relevant entity, relation,
evidence requirement, conclusion, output property, or behavioral intention
that is present in the right state but weak or absent in the left state.

Required output representation:
Return `mid_output` as one non-empty string containing a summary1 output.

response:
{
  "mid_output": "First-hop evidence: Hop1 returned a Sock-Cess page stating: "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release." The band’s 1984 debut page (The Red Hot Chili Peppers) also shows an EMI association.

Bridge / missing fact for next hop: Mother's Milk is also a 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers release (e.g., contains the song "Good Time Boys"), but the current snippets do not show what label or territories Mother's Milk was released on. We must confirm whether the question intends the EMI UK promotional compilation (Sock-Cess) or the 1989 studio album Mother's Milk and whether Mother's Milk has any EMI release credit.

Compact next-hop retrieval directions: open the Sock-Cess page lede/infobox to confirm release date, label, region, and promotional vs. standard status; open the Mother's Milk page to check its release year and label credits; if ambiguity remains, open the band discography (Albums section) to reconcile which 1989 album would be the canonical EMI-associated release.

Do not assume a final answer here—next hop should resolve which 1989 release the question refers to and verify label/territory details.",
  "rationale": "This midpoint preserves the left state's core finding that Sock-Cess is a 1989 EMI UK promotional compilation, but it incorporates the right state's key missing-bridge concern: Mother's Milk is another 1989 release and its label/territory attribution is not yet confirmed. It therefore adds the minimal, task-relevant uncertainty and directs retrieval to open Sock-Cess and Mother's Milk pages (and discography if needed). This is a smaller, concrete functional step toward the right state (introducing the need to verify Mother's Milk's label) without adopting the fuller set of retrieval instructions and checks the right state contains."
}
